name: Project.Documentation.Consolidation.Feature_IncrementalConsolidation
observations: Incremental documentation consolidation workflow: rescan new docs since last consolidation, identify clusters, integrate into existing core docs rather than create new ones when topic overlap exists. Enables continuous documentation maintenance without full re-consolidation.

Implementation: 18 new docs (15 implementation summaries + 3 technical) consolidated into 3 targets: 1 new comprehensive doc (IMPL_bstool_evolution 832 lines from 8 summaries) + 2 enhanced core docs (TECH_codegraph_system +327 lines, ARCH_command_system +367 lines). Archive rate 20.5% (15 archived).

Integration strategy: (A) Timeline-based evolution narrative for iterative implementations, (B) Section enhancement for related technical content, (C) Advanced patterns section for architectural extensions. Result: 59% content reduction (3,6841,526 lines) while preserving all knowledge.
created:2025-10-11,modified:2025-10-11,refs:0

name: Project.Documentation.Consolidation.Pattern_EvolutionaryDocumentation
observations: Pattern: Consolidate iterative implementation summaries into single comprehensive evolution narrative showing feature maturation timeline. Use chronological phases (Phase 1Phase 5) with problemsolutionresults structure per phase.

Application: BsTool integration (Jan-Oct 2025): 8 implementation summaries (sequential execution, file integration, node suffix, color updates, deferred execution, complete fix) consolidated into IMPL_bstool_evolution.md with 5-phase timeline + testing + cross-references.

Benefits: Single source of truth for feature history, easier onboarding (see evolution path), reduced duplication (64% reduction 1846832 lines), comprehensive testing section (23 tests documented), maintains all technical details while improving narrative flow.
created:2025-10-11,modified:2025-10-11,refs:0

name: Project.Documentation.Consolidation.Method_CoreDocEnhancement
observations: Method: Enhance existing core documents with new sections rather than create new docs when topic already covered. Add 3-5 sections maintaining document structure (metadata, TOC, sections, cross-refs). Preserve existing content integrity.

Implementation: TECH_codegraph_system enhanced with 3 sections (Cluster Layer Enhancement, DevTeam Integration Patterns, Documentation Pointer System) adding 327 lines. ARCH_command_system enhanced with Advanced Execution Patterns section (5 subsections) adding 367 lines.

Decision criteria: Create new doc if distinct topic/audience, enhance existing if related topic/extends coverage. BsTool evolution warranted new doc (distinct implementation history), codegraph/command enhancements fit existing docs (related technical/architectural content).
created:2025-10-11,modified:2025-10-11,refs:0

name: Project.Documentation.Consolidation.Pattern_ClusterBasedConsolidation
observations: Pattern: Group documents by topic clusters (bstool/codegraph/commands) rather than timestamp for logical organization. Identify consolidation opportunities within clusters: 8 bstool docs1 evolution doc, 3 codegraph docsintegrate existing, 4 command docsenhance architecture.

Clustering strategy: (A) Iterative implementations of same featureevolution narrative, (B) Technical enhancements of same systemsection additions, (C) Related architectural patternsadvanced patterns section. Each cluster gets distinct consolidation approach based on content type.

Results: 3 consolidation clusters processed with 59% overall reduction (3,6841,526 lines), 15 docs archived, 100% template compliance maintained, <2% cross-document duplication achieved. Archive strategy: preserve originals in docs/archive/implementation/2025-01/ for historical reference.
created:2025-10-11,modified:2025-10-11,refs:0

name: Project.Documentation.Consolidation.Workflow_UpdateDocuments
observations: Workflow: update_documents.md 10-phase interleaved consolidation (Phase 0 planning, Phases 1-9 analysisimplementation). Applied incrementally: rescan new docs, identify clusters, execute targeted consolidation without full re-processing. Adapted for continuous maintenance.

Execution: PLANREMEMBERASSESSANALYZEARCHITECTIMPLEMENTTESTLEARNDOCUMENTLOG. DevTeam orchestrator pattern with CEPH tracking (Current:73 docs Expected:58 docs Problem:integrate 18 new efficiently). Validation: template compliance, cross-references, duplication analysis, archive verification.

Metrics: Before 73 docs (18 core+55 workflow), After 58 docs (19 core+29 implementation+7 analysis+2 navigation) + 15 archived. Consolidation ratio 15:1 archived, content efficiency 59% reduction, template compliance 100%, cross-refs +8 new links, duplication <2%.
created:2025-10-11,modified:2025-10-11,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.BsTool.Feature_DeferredExecutionTimingFix
observations: Fixed signal timing race condition in deferred BsTool execution (Oct 12 2025). Root cause: 29-line gap between command_completed signal emission (line 372) and _is_processing flag reset (lines 401-404) in command_queue.py caused premature queue idle checks. Solution: QTimer.singleShot(50ms) delay pattern in handle_command_completed() before checking queue state. Architecture: Signal emits → 29 lines of code execute → flag resets → QTimer fires 50ms later → check executes → finds queue idle correctly.

Integration: Modified node_tree_presenter.py handle_command_completed() lines 403-409 with QTimer.singleShot(50, self._check_and_execute_pending_bstool), added new helper method _check_and_execute_pending_bstool() lines 506-524 to perform delayed queue state validation.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.BsTool.Method_CheckAndExecutePendingBstool
observations: Helper method added to node_tree_presenter.py (lines 506-524) implementing delayed queue idle check pattern for deferred BsTool execution. Called via QTimer.singleShot(50ms) from handle_command_completed() to allow async state transitions. Architecture: Validates _pending_bstool exists → checks command_queue.is_processing → calls _execute_pending_bstool() only when queue confirmed idle. Includes comprehensive logging for all decision paths.

Implementation details: No parameters (uses self._pending_bstool dict state), returns None, logs all paths (no pending/queue busy/executing), critical for sequential workflow coordination between FBC/RPC commands and BsTool execution.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Global.PyQt5.Pattern_EventLoopDelayForAsyncState
observations: QTimer.singleShot(delay_ms, callback) pattern allows Qt event loop to process pending signals/slots before dependent checks execute. Critical for signal-driven architectures where state changes happen asynchronously across multiple methods. Use when: Signal emission and state transition are separated by code execution (not atomic), immediate checks would see stale state, need to guarantee async state synchronization. Pattern: Signal emits → intervening code executes → QTimer schedules callback → event loop processes queued signals → callback executes with fresh state.

PyQt5 application: command_completed signal → 29 lines of worker cleanup → _is_processing flag reset → QTimer.singleShot(50) → event loop tick → delayed check sees correct flag state. 50ms delay empirically validated (user test showed 48ms actual delay). Alternative: Manual event processing with QCoreApplication.processEvents() less reliable for deep signal chains.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.BsTool.Pattern_SignalBasedCompletion
observations: BsToolCommandService uses PyQt signal architecture for async completion notification instead of callbacks. Signal: bstool_execution_completed = pyqtSignal(str, bool, int) emits log_file_path, success, return_code. Connected in node_tree_presenter.__init__() line 108 to _handle_bstool_completed() handler. This decouples caller from completion handling - execute_bstool() accepts only 2 params (log_file_path, bstool_command_args), no callback needed.

Anti-pattern caught: Initial implementation incorrectly passed callback= and log_token= params causing TypeError. Signals eliminate callback parameter pollution in method signatures. Pattern benefits: Decouples execution from handling, allows multiple handlers via signal connections, follows Qt async execution model, cleaner API surface.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.BsTool.Feature_DeferredExecutionTimingFix

Solution: QTimer.singleShot(50ms) delay pattern in handle_command_completed() before checking queue state. Architecture: Signal emits → 29 lines of code execute → flag resets → QTimer fires 50ms later → check executes → finds queue idle correctly.

Integration: Modified node_tree_presenter.py handle_command_completed() lines 403-409 with QTimer.singleShot(50, self._check_and_execute_pending_bstool), added new helper method _check_and_execute_pending_bstool() lines 506-524 to perform delayed queue state validation.

name: Project.Commander.Command.Feature_BsToolSignalForwarding
observations: Fixed sequential processing continuation after BsTool execution by implementing signal forwarding from BsToolCommandService to CommandQueue.
Root cause: BsToolWorker submitted directly to thread pool bypassed CommandQueue.start_processing(), missing automatic signal forwarding that CommandWorker (FBC/RPC) receives.

Solution: Added _handle_worker_completed() method that forwards command_completed signal to CommandQueue, enabling NodeTreePresenter.handle_command_completed() to fire and continue sequential workflow.

Signal chain: BsToolWorker.signals.command_completed → BsToolCommandService._handle_worker_completed → CommandQueue.command_completed.emit → NodeTreePresenter.handle_command_completed → _check_sequential_processing_continuation.
Integration: Part of unified queue architecture ensuring FBC/RPC/LOG commands execute sequentially with proper continuation logic.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.Command.Method_HandleWorkerCompleted
observations: CRITICAL signal forwarding method in BsToolCommandService that bridges BsToolWorker and CommandQueue for sequential processing.
Implementation: Receives command_completed from BsToolWorker, forwards to CommandQueue via self.command_queue.command_completed.emit(command, result, success, token).
Without this method: Sequential processing stops after first BsTool execution because NodeTreePresenter never receives completion signal.
Backward compatibility: Also emits legacy bstool_execution_completed signal for UI components not yet migrated to queue architecture.
Debug logging: Added explicit log '_handle_worker_completed: Forwarding signal to CommandQueue' for troubleshooting signal flow issues.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.Command.Pattern_QRunnableSignalBridging
observations: Architectural pattern for bridging QRunnable workers with PyQt signal systems when workers bypass normal processing queues.
Problem: QRunnable workers submitted directly to QThreadPool don't automatically integrate with parent queue's signal forwarding mechanisms.
Solution: Service layer (BsToolCommandService) acts as signal bridge by connecting to worker signals (_handle_worker_completed) and re-emitting on parent queue's signals.
Signal flow: Worker.signals → Service.handler → Queue.signals → Presenter.handler, creating complete signal chain despite direct thread pool submission.
Alternative considered: Route BsToolWorker through CommandQueue.start_processing() like CommandWorker, rejected due to different execution model (synchronous subprocess vs async telnet).
Applicable to: Any QRunnable worker that needs to participate in sequential processing workflows but can't use standard queue submission patterns.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.Sequential.Feature_TabSwitchingAutomation
observations: Automatic tab switching during sequential execution (Print All Nodes). When FBC/RPC commands execute, Telnet tab automatically switches to foreground to display command output.

Implementation: switch_to_telnet_tab_signal emitted from NodeTreePresenter.handle_command_completed() alongside command_output_display_signal for FBC/RPC token types. Connected to lambda: self.session_tabs.setCurrentWidget(self.telnet_tab) in CommanderWindow._connect_signals().

Integration: Unifies user experience between manual execution (single command) and sequential execution (Print All Nodes workflow). Both paths now provide identical visual feedback with automatic tab switching.

name: Project.Commander.Sequential.Method_SwitchToTelnetTabSignal
observations: Signal emission method in NodeTreePresenter for automatic tab switching. Emits switch_to_telnet_tab_signal (pyqtSignal) when FBC/RPC commands complete during sequential execution.
Implementation: Lines 392-397 in node_tree_presenter.py. Checks token.token_type in ['FBC','RPC'] and result exists before emitting. Paired with command_output_display_signal emission.

Signal flow: NodeTreePresenter.handle_command_completed() → switch_to_telnet_tab_signal.emit() → CommanderWindow lambda connection → session_tabs.setCurrentWidget(telnet_tab). Provides instant visual feedback.

name: Project.Commander.Sequential.Method_HandleSequentialOutput
observations: Output routing handler in CommanderWindow._handle_sequential_output(). Routes sequential execution command results to appropriate tabs based on token type.

Implementation: Lines 445-459 in commander_window.py. Token routing logic: FBC/RPC → telnet_tab.append_output(output_text), LOG → pass (BsTool handles via bstool_output_signal). Connected to command_output_display_signal from NodeTreePresenter.

Integration: Receives (output_text: str, token_type: str) parameters. Preserves separation of concerns - BsTool service independently handles LOG output via temp file reading, this method only handles Telnet-based output display.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.Sequential.Pattern_DualExecutionPathUnification
observations: Pattern for unifying manual and sequential execution paths to provide identical user experience. Addresses divergence where manual execution had output display but sequential execution lacked it.

Approach: Identified signal path gap (command_queue.command_completed carried result but nothing displayed it). Solution: Added parallel signal emission (command_output_display_signal + switch_to_telnet_tab_signal) alongside existing completion signals. Routing handler dispatches to correct UI components.

Architecture: Preserves existing BsTool workflow (temp file reading via bstool_output_signal), only adds missing Telnet display path. Uses token type discrimination (FBC/RPC vs LOG) to route output correctly. Signal-based loose coupling enables clean separation.
created:2025-10-12,modified:2025-10-12,refs:0

name: Project.Commander.BugFix.Feature_SequentialUIHighlightTiming
observations: Fixed premature UI highlight jump during sequential execution. Moved highlight from process_node_print_commands to _check_sequential_processing_continuation.
Root cause: Synchronous highlight fired before async BsTool completed, causing visual jump to next node.
Solution: Deferred highlight to async continuation point after queue confirms idle state.

name: Project.Commander.BugFix.Feature_SequentialBsToolOutputDisplay
observations: Fixed missing BsTool output during sequential execution. BsToolWorker now stores actual output in self.result, _handle_worker_completed emits bstool_output_signal.
Root cause: self.result contained summary message instead of actual output, bstool_output_signal never emitted during sequential mode.

Solution: Changed self.result to '
'.join(output_lines), added signal emission with header separator for each node.

name: Project.Commander.Pattern.DynamicTabSwitching
observations: Dynamic tab switching during sequential execution: FBC/RPCTelnet, LOGBsTool. Provides real-time visibility of command output.
Implementation: handle_command_completed checks token.token_type and emits appropriate switch signal.
Benefit: User sees each command's output as it executes, improves UX during Print All Nodes workflow.

name: Project.Commander.Method.BsToolCommandService_HandleWorkerCompleted

name: Project.Commander.Method.NodeTreePresenter_CheckSequentialProcessingContinuation

name: Project.Feature.Connection.PrintAllNodesAutoConnect
observations: Auto-connect before Print All Nodes: checks/establishes Telnet debugger connection before sequential execution

name: Project.Pattern.Validation.EarlyPrerequisiteCheck
observations: Pattern: Validate prerequisites before multi-step workflow to avoid partial failure
Example: Check Telnet connection before Print All Nodes prevents mid-workflow errors
Benefits: Clear user feedback, no wasted processing, consistent state
